title: SLO (fleet schema; goodput reference for Contracts 3 and 7)
description: A named, versioned set of objectives. benchmark-result.goodput.slo_ref and capacity-recommendation.inputs.slo_ref point at instances of this schema — goodput is only defined relative to one. PROVENANCE IS MANDATORY per objective (basis + date; no fabricated defaults). NORMATIVE declaration rule, encoded structurally: scope=model-serving SLOs (TTFT/ITL/goodput and other model-level targets) are declared ONLY from measurement — every objective's provenance.basis MUST be 'measured' (with the measurement identified in provenance.source); declaring model-level targets in advance of measurement is not schema-valid. scope=gateway SLOs may carry source-reported program targets (as of 2026-07 — re-baseline from measurement if infeasible).

version.description: Consumers reference SLOs by id + version; results computed against different SLO versions are not comparable.

scope.description: gateway: targets on gateway-owned signals (overhead, cancellation propagation, settlement, control-plane actions). model-serving: targets on model-level signals (TTFT, ITL, e2e, stall, goodput) — measurement-derived only.

applies_to.description: What this SLO is scoped to (informative; matching is by reference, not by these fields).

signal.description: Signal name. For model-serving scope use Contract 2 vocabulary names (e.g. ttft_seconds, itl_seconds, e2e_duration_seconds, max_stall_seconds; client_ prefix for client-side series). Gateway-target signals: gateway_overhead_non_queue_seconds, cancellation_propagation_seconds, usage_settle_variance_ratio, key_revocation_effect_seconds, config_publish_effect_seconds.

statistic.description: 'value' = the signal is a scalar per evaluation window (e.g. a variance ratio or propagation deadline), not a per-request distribution.

notes.description: Objective-specific conditions (e.g. cancellation propagation target holds on the gateway+mock path).

basis.description: Model-level SLOs are declared only from measurement, never in advance.

provenance.description: MANDATORY provenance: how this objective's threshold was established and when. basis=measured: from an identified measurement (source = benchmark result_id or measurement artifact). basis=source-reported: from an identified external source (e.g. the program plan's source-verified gateway targets). basis=assumed: an explicit assumption — never a silent default; state the reasoning in notes.

source.description: REQUIRED for measured and source-reported values: what produced or reported the value.
